prompt: I need to add OAuth login, update the user model, and migrate the sessions table. Where do we start?
note: clear multi-step task -> should trigger planning; mentions a table migration but is not migration-shaped, so migration-discipline must not fire

prompt: Refactor the payment service onto the new gateway; it touches about eight files.
note: no hint, but 3+ steps

prompt: That is the second time this session the executor subagent has forgotten to run the verify command after an edit. I have not said anything about it before now.
note: repeated agent mistake, no user correction phrasing

prompt: Look across all my installed skills and tell me which recurring principles are not in my rules file yet.
note: cross-skill principle promotion

prompt: This is a 15-year-old codebase with unusual conventions. Make future edits match the existing style instead of drifting toward mainstream idioms.
note: codify implicit legacy conventions

prompt: I just finished the research phase and I am about to start coding. Is now a good moment to clear out context?
note: boundary: conversation growth (compact) vs config cost (budget)

prompt: My agent config feels heavy and sessions start slow. Which skills or instructions cost the most tokens every single turn?
note: boundary: always-on config cost, not conversation

prompt: After adding five new skills, do a hygiene pass on how much they cost to keep loaded.
note: boundary: cost hygiene vs rule promotion

prompt: I added a rule that the agent must reproduce a bug as a failing test first. In fresh sessions, is that rule actually being obeyed?
note: boundary: is a fired rule FOLLOWED (comply)

prompt: When I describe a task, is the correct skill actually getting picked, or is the wrong one firing?
note: boundary: is the RIGHT skill PICKED (activation)

prompt: Our repo already documents its conventions and we use GitHub's Copilot review on pull requests. Turn those rules into review directives so the bot flags violations instead of applying generic defaults.
note: boundary: generate Copilot PR-review files from documented conventions, not capture implicit legacy style

prompt: This next feature is going to touch a lot of files and needs a real plan before anyone writes code. What's the plan?
note: boundary: starting a task's own tasks/todo.md workflow (plan-and-track) vs picking which subagent tier should draft it (efficient-frontier)

prompt: Polish this README section before I publish it, it reads like ChatGPT wrote it: lots of em dashes and phrases like 'stands as a testament'.
note: AI-writing-tell cleanup on user-facing prose

prompt: We are porting the billing service from Python to Go, file by file, across the whole repo. I want to split the work across four parallel agents, each owning a different set of modules, and keep behavior identical to the old implementation. How do we set this up?
note: boundary: migration-shaped work routes to migration-discipline; plan-and-track co-firing is legitimate layering, and plan-multistep guards the reverse direction

prompt: Continue the migration: yesterday's Java-to-Kotlin port of the billing service left about 40 type errors in the service layer. Pick up where we left off and work through them.
note: resume trigger: mid-migration continuation must re-fire the skill; previously live-validated but never corpus-guarded

prompt: Threat model this repo: enumerate the trust boundaries, the abuse paths an attacker could take, and write up a prioritized threat model doc.
note: explicit AppSec threat-modeling request over a repo/path

prompt: Before I touch hooks/gateguard.js and hooks/delivery-gate.js again: is keeping them as two separate hooks the right call, or should they be merged into one? Get me an independent design review on that boundary before I commit to either.
note: boundary: general architecture/design-tradeoff review routes to the architect-reviewer tier, not an explicit AppSec threat-modeling ask

prompt: The TypeScript 5.9 upgrade left about 2,300 type errors across the monorepo. Work through all of them mechanically without changing behavior.
note: large-error-set clause: mechanical many-file sweep without port/migration wording routes via the error-set trigger

prompt: Create a PR for the fix on this branch, request a copilot review, resolve the review comments, then squash and merge.
note: explicit imperative instruction enumerating 3+ steps still routes to planning; tracking the steps is not re-litigating them

prompt: Great, that one-line fix works. While you're in there, also update the changelog, add a regression test for it, and open a release PR.
note: mid-session escalation: a task that was trivial grows to 3+ steps, and the growth moment counts as the start
